methods.name: Stage-Progression

data.narrative:
AstroForge is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $53M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 4 private peers at the same or adjacent stage (ClearSpace, Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network), with valuations ranging from $400M to $2.0B, as well as reference to 5 public Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) that trade at a median P/S of 36.9x.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $53M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a standard 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implies $106M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 comparable companies yields an implied valuation of $850M, based on the median of the range $400M–$2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because AstroForge's revenue is not disclosed.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $90M to $977M, with a median of $478M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty about where AstroForge sits relative to its peer set. The stage-progression method implies the lower bound, while peer comparables suggest significantly higher valuations. Key uncertainties include technology maturity relative to Series B-stage peers, near-term revenue trajectory, and capital intensity of AstroForge's operational roadmap relative to the comparable companies.
